title: 第 59 節
en: Such concessions are found in other authoritative works also, as, for instance, in the Vishmi-PuraTia, I, 17, 83 : —
( 0 Daitya, these various opinions have I declared for those who admit a difference (who are not yet monists), by making a concession (to dualism). Let this abstract of mine be listened to.'

Nay it is possible that in some accredited systems also opinions should have been put forward in contradiction with the Veda in order to shut out bad men from a knowledge of the truth. Such parts would of course not be means of right knowledge, but the other and principal parts only, which are in harmony with /Sruti and Smriti. Hence we see that in the Padma-Pur^a fault is found with THE SIX SYSTEMS. 457 all systems except the Brahma-Mimams& and Yoga. Here we see God (Siva,} saying to Parvati : —
f Listen, 0 goddess, I shall in succession tell you the heretical theories by the mere hearing of which even sages lose their knowledge.

First of all, I myself have taught the $aiva, Pasupata and other systems, and afterwards others have been promulgated by Brahmans, who were filled by my powers. KaTiada has promulgated the great Vaiseshika doctrine, Gautama the Nyaya, Kapila the Samkhya.- The Brahman (raimini has composed a very large work of atheistic character, the first of the two Mimamsas, which treat of the meaning of the Veda. Then, in order to destroy the demons, Dhisha'^a (Brihaspati) propounded the altogether despicable Jfarvaka system ; and Vishnu, under the disguise of Buddha, propounded the erroneous Bauddha system which teaches that people are to go naked, and should wear blue or other coloured garments, while I myself, 0 goddess, under the disguise of a Brahman (i.e. of $a??ikara) have taught in this Kali age the doctrine of illusion (Maya) which is false and only a disguised Buddhism. It is spread far and wide in the world, and attributes a false meaning to the words of the Veda. In it it is said that all works should be relinquished, and after surrendering all works, comp]ete inactivity is recommended.

I have taught in it the identity of the highest Self and 'the individual Self, and have represented the highest form of Brahman as entirely free from qualities; and this in order to destroy the whole world in this Kali age. This extensive, non-Vedic, deceptive doctrine has been propounded by me, as if it presented the true meaning of the Veda, in order that all living things might perish/ All this and more has been explained by me in the commentary on the Brahma-Mimamsa, and it is wrong therefore to say of any of the admittedly orthodox systems of philosophy that it is not the means of right knowledge or that it is refuted by others. For in reality none of them is contradicted or refuted in what constitutes its own chief object.
But, if it be asked whether the Samkhya-philosophy 4-58 INDIAN PHILOSOPHY.

has not likewise made a mere concession with regard to the multiplicity of souls, we answer decidedly, No. For on that point there is really no contradiction (between the two, Samkhya and Vedanta) because it is shown in the chapter which begins at Brahma-Sutras II, 3, 43, and declares that the individual self is a part of the Highest Self, because the multiplicity is stated (in the Veda) ; that the Brahma-M$ma?Tisa also recognises a multiplicity of Atman. But that the individual souls, as conceived by the Samkhya, are Atman is certainly denied by the Vedanta, for it follows from Sutra IV, i, 3 : ' They know him and teach him as Atman/ that to the Vedantins, from the standpoint of absolute truth, the highest soul only is Atman. Nevertheless the Samkhya does not thereby lose its authoritative character, because it is not superseded by the Vedanta in what constitutes its own characteristic doctrine, namely that for the individual soul, the knowledge of its being different from everything else, constitutes the true means of liberation. There is no contradiction therefore, because the concepts of the manifold Atman and of the one Atman, so well known from Veda and tradition, can be fully reconciled according as we take an empirical or metaphysical view, as has been explained by ourselves in the Commentary on the Brahma-Mimams^ — Sapienti sat"

I have given here this long extract from Vi^/7anaBhikshu, though I have to confess that in several places the thread of the argument is difficult to follow, even after the care bestowed on disentangling it by Professor Garbe. Still, even as it is, it will be useful, I hope, as a good specimen of the Indian way of carrying on a philosophical controversy. Nay, in spite of all that has been said against Vi#;?ana-Bhikshu, I cannot deny that to a certain extent he seems to me right in discerning a kind of unity behind the variety of the various philosophical systems, each being regarded as a step towards the highest and final truth. He certainly helps us to understand how it came to pass that the followers of systems which to our mind seem directly opposed to each other on very important points, managed to keep peace with each other and with the Veda, THE SIX SYSTEMS. 459 the highest authority in all matters religious, philosophical and moral. The idea that the largely accepted interpretation of the Vedanta-Sutras by $amkara was a perversion of the Veda and of BMaraya-tt/a's Sutras, not much better than Buddhism, nay that Buddhism was the work of Vishnu, intended for the destruction of unbelievers, is very extraordinary, and evidently of late origin. Nay, nothing seems to me to show better that these Pura^as, in the form in which we possess them, are of recent origin, and certainly not the outcome of a period previous to the Renaissance of Sanskrit literature, than passages like those quoted by Vi$/7ana-Bhikshu, representing the gods of the modern Hindu pantheon as interfering with the ancient philosophy of India, and propounding views which they know to be erroneous with the intention of deceiving mankind. Whatever the age of our philosophical Sutras may be, and some of them, in the form in which we possess them, are certainly more modern than our Pur&nas, yet the tradition or Parampara which they represent must be much older; and in trying to enter into the spirit of the Six Systems, we must implicitly trust to their guidance, without allowing ourselves to be disturbed by the fancies of later sects, INDEX.

zh: 在其他權威著作中也可發現類似的讓步之詞，例如在《毗濕奴往世書》（Vishnu-Purana）I, 17, 83中：——
（引自《奧義書》）「啊，大地之神啊，為了那些承認差異者（尚未皈依一元論者），我藉由對二元論的讓步，宣示了這些種種觀點。願汝傾聽我這簡明之述。」

而且有可能在某些得到認可的體系中，為了將惡人排除在真理知識之外，也提出了與《吠陀》相悖的意見。當然，這些部分不會是正確知識的手段，只有與《斯魯提》和《史摩利提》相和諧的另一些部分，以及主要部分才是如此。因此，我們在《帕德瑪往世書》（Padma-Purāṇa）中看到對六大體系中的所有體系都有駁斥，除了《梵我論》（Brahma-Mimāmsā）和《瑜伽》體系外。在這裡我們看到上帝（濕婆）向帕瓦蒂說：──
聽著吧，0 女神，我將依次為你述說那些異端學說，即使賢者僅僅聽聞這些學說便會喪失其知識。

首先，我本人教導了濕婆（Siva）、帕蘇帕塔（Pasupata）及其他體系，其後其他體系乃由被我之力所充滿的婆羅門所倡立。迦帝耶陀（KaTiada）倡立了偉大的勝論（Vaiseshika）教義，喬答摩（Gautama）倡立了因明（Nyaya），迦毘羅（Kapila）倡立了數論（Samkhya）。婆羅門吉彌尼（Jaimini）撰著了一部性質為無神論的巨著，乃對吠陀（Veda）之義義進行討論的兩種彌曼差（Mimamsa）中的第一種。隨後，為了摧毀諸魔鬼，迪沙那（Brihaspati）倡立了完全可鄙的順世論（Carvaka）體系；而毗濕奴（Vishnu）以佛陀（Buddha）之化身倡立了謬誤的佛教（Bauddha）體系，其教導人們應當赤身裸體，應當穿著藍色或其他色彩的衣服。而我本人，噢女神啊，以婆羅門（即商羯羅 Sankara）之化身，在此劫濁（Kali age）教導了幻相（Maya）之教義，其乃虛妄之說，只是變裝的佛教。它在世上廣泛傳播，並對吠陀之言辭賦予虛妄之義。在其中聲言所有行為應當被棄捨，在放棄了所有行為之後，乃推薦完全的無為。

我在其中教導最高的自我與個別自我的同一性，並將梵的最高形式表現為完全不具有任何性質；我這樣做是為了在劫末時代摧毀整個世界。這種廣泛的、非吠陀的、欺騙性的教說已被我提出，彷彿它呈現了吠陀的真正意義，目的是讓所有生物都滅亡。所有這些乃至更多的內容已被我在《梵經評疏》（Brahma-Mimamsa）的評注中加以闡明，因此說任何公認的正統哲學體系不是正確知識的手段，或者說它被他人駁斥，這是錯誤的。實際上，沒有任何一個體系在構成其自身主要對象的事物上受到矛盾或駁斥。
但如果有人問起數論哲學（Samkhya-philosophy）是否……

對於眾多靈魂的問題，吾等決然否定其亦為單純之讓步。蓋在此點實無真正矛盾（數論派與吠檀多派之間），因梵經註疏第二篇第三章第四十三經所示，個我乃最高自我之一部分，蓋吠陀中已明言其多數性；梵經義疏亦認可阿特曼（Atman）之多數性。然個靈魂如數論派所設想者乃阿特曼，吠檀多派實否認之，蓋由經文第四篇第一章第三經而生：「彼等知之並以阿特曼教之」，對吠檀多派而言，自絕對真理之立場，唯有最高靈魂乃為阿特曼。然數論派因此不喪失其權威性，蓋其特有教義中吠檀多派並未廢黜之，即個靈魂認知其有異於一切他物之知識，此乃解脫之真實手段。故無矛盾存焉，因吠陀與傳統所熟知之眾阿特曼與一阿特曼之概念，依吾等視之為經驗性或形而上學觀點之異，可完全調和，如吾等於梵經義疏註釋中所示——智者不煩言也。

我在此引用了維亞那·比克舒（Vijñana-Bhikshu）的這段長篇節錄，儘管我必須坦承，即使在葛貝教授（Professor Garbe）細心解纏之後，論證的思路在許多地方仍難以追蹤。然而，即便如此，我希望它將是有用的，足以作為印度人進行哲學論辯的方式之一個好範例。更有甚者，儘管有許多針對維亞那·比克舒的批評，我仍不能否認，在某種程度上，他似乎是對的，他在各種哲學體系的多樣性背後辨識出某種統一性，每一個體系都被視為邁向最高及最終真理的一個階段。他確實幫助我們理解，為何那些在我們看來在許多重要問題上似乎彼此直接對立的體系的追隨者，能夠彼此保持和平，並與《吠陀》——在所有宗教、哲學及道德事務上的最高權威——相安無事。認為商羯羅（Śaṃkara）廣為接受的《韋丹塔經論》（Vedanta-Sutras）詮釋乃是對《吠陀》及婆羅摩笈多（Bhārata-tt/a）之《經論》（Sutras）的歪曲，不遠勝於佛教，甚至說佛教乃是毗濕奴的傑作，旨在摧毀不信者——這個想法實在是極其離奇，顯然也是後出的。事實上，沒有什麼比維亞那·比克舒所引用的那些段落更能彰顯：這些《往世書》（Purāṇas）以我們所擁有的形式乃是近期的產物，確實不是梵文文學復興時期之前的時代的成果——這些段落將現代印度教萬神殿的眾神描繪成干預印度古代哲學，並提出他們知道是錯誤的觀點，意圖欺騙人類。無論我們哲學《經論》的年代何時，其中有些在我們所擁有的形式上確實比我們的《往世書》更為現代，然而它們所代表的傳統或師資相承（Paramparā）必定古老得多；在試圖進入六大體系的精神時，我們必須隱然相信它們的指引，不應允許自己為後世宗派的幻想所擾亂。
